name: Image Database Manager (PyQt5)
description: PyQt5 GUI that ingests JSON image records, builds dynamic tables, resolves folder paths across naming conventions, and opens matching directories—with validation and path hygiene before shelling out to the OS file manager.
longDescription: Users maintain a JSON-defined image catalog; the app renders sortable, filterable tables, builds candidate folder paths using several naming strategies (space, underscore, concatenated, model-only), checks that paths exist, and opens the system file manager when a match is found. JSON sources are validated before filesystem operations; candidate paths are checked to reduce traversal surprises when data is messy or user-edited. Settings persist in `settings.json`; optional packaged builds bundle icons and backgrounds.
features: JSON ingestion to dynamic tables with validation before FS access
Multiple folder naming strategies for messy on-disk conventions
Error surfacing when paths are missing or JSON is malformed
Optional build script with asset bundling
Defensive normalisation of user-provided path components
highlights: Solves messy on-disk naming conventions with explicit strategies
Clear validation messages when paths or data are invalid
challenges: Filesystem assumptions break when assets move between drives or OS locales.
solutions: Configurable settings, resilient path checks, and clear errors before shelling out to Explorer/Finder.
security: Never publish decryption passwords in public documentation or marketing material
Validate JSON sources before executing filesystem operations
Sanitize and normalise paths to avoid traversal surprises from untrusted catalog edits
Open-in-FM actions only after existence checks—no blind shelling to arbitrary strings
Treat shared JSON catalogs as untrusted input when they originate from third parties
